questions.text: What is the recurring refrain that follows the story of every Prophet mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not believers. And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

questions.text: How did Allah comfort the Prophet (SAW) regarding his grief over the disbelievers' rejection?
questions.answer: He asked if the Prophet would kill himself with grief because they do not believe, and reminded him that if He willed, He could send a sign from heaven that would force them to submit.

questions.text: What was the fear Musa (AS) expressed when Allah commanded him to go to Pharaoh?
questions.answer: He feared they would deny him, that his breast would be straitened, his tongue would not be fluent, and they had a charge of a crime against him (killing an Egyptian).

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's personal argument against Musa (AS) when he first arrived?
questions.answer: He reminded Musa that they had raised him as a child among them and accused him of being ungrateful for the 'favor' they did for him.

questions.text: How did Musa (AS) define the 'Lord of the worlds' to Pharaoh?
questions.answer: The Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of your forefathers, and the Lord of the East and the West.

questions.text: What was the 'miracle of the staff' as described in the contest with the sorcerers?
questions.answer: It turned into a real serpent and swallowed up the falsehoods (illusions) that the sorcerers produced.

questions.text: What did the sorcerers say to Pharaoh after they were threatened with crucifixion?
questions.answer: They said 'No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return. We hope our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers.'

questions.text: What command was given to Musa (AS) when Pharaoh and his army pursued the Children of Israel to the sea?
questions.answer: 'Strike the sea with your staff.' It parted, and each part was like a great towering mountain.

questions.text: What happened to Pharaoh's army after the Children of Israel crossed the sea?
questions.answer: Allah drowned the other party (the Egyptians) completely.

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) ask his father and his people regarding what they worshipped?
questions.answer: He asked, 'Do they hear you when you supplicate? Or do they benefit you or harm you?'

questions.text: Describe Ibrahim's (AS) description of his Lord in his debate with his people.
questions.answer: He described Allah as the One who created him, guides him, feeds him, gives him drink, heals him when sick, and will cause him to die then bring him to life.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) prayer regarding his 'mention' in the future?
questions.answer: He asked Allah to 'grant me a reputation of honor (Lisan Sidq) among later generations'.

questions.text: Who will be saved on the Day of Judgment according to Ibrahim's (AS) prayer?
questions.answer: Only those who come to Allah with a 'Qalbun Salim' (a sound and pure heart).

questions.text: What did the 'lowest' people of Nuh's nation say to him regarding his followers?
questions.answer: They said, 'Shall we believe in you while the lowest [of people] follow you?'

questions.text: How did Nuh (AS) respond to his people's request to drive away the poor believers?
questions.answer: He said their account is only with his Lord and he is not one to drive away the believers.

questions.text: What was the punishment for the people of Nuh mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: They were drowned, while Nuh and those with him were saved in the laden ship.

questions.text: What did the people of 'Ad take pride in building as monuments?
questions.answer: Signs/buildings on every high place for amusement and strongholds as if they would live forever.

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people of 'Ad?
questions.answer: Prophet Hud (AS).

questions.text: How did Hud (AS) describe the people of 'Ad's behavior when they seized people?
questions.answer: He said they seized people as tyrants (Jabbarin).

questions.text: What was the punishment for the people of 'Ad?
questions.answer: They were destroyed, and Allah says in that is a sign, but most were not believers.

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people of Thamud?
questions.answer: Prophet Salih (AS).

questions.text: What were the people of Thamud skilled at building?
questions.answer: Hewing houses out of the mountains with great skill.

questions.text: What was the miraculous sign given to the people of Thamud?
questions.answer: A She-Camel which had a designated day for drinking water, as did the people.

questions.text: What happened after the people of Thamud hamstrung the She-Camel?
questions.answer: A punishment (shriek/earthquake) seized them and they became corpses in their homes.

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people who committed immorality never before seen?
questions.answer: Prophet Lut (AS).

questions.text: What was the people of Lut's reaction to his warnings?
questions.answer: They threatened to expel him from the city because he and his followers were 'people who keep themselves pure'.

questions.text: What was the punishment for the people of Lut in this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah rained upon them a rain of stones, destroying all except Lut and his family (excluding his wife).

questions.text: Who were the 'People of the Wood' (Ashab al-Aykah)?
questions.answer: The people of Prophet Shu'ayb (AS).

questions.text: What economic commands did Shu'ayb give his people?
questions.answer: To give full measure, weigh with an even balance, and not deprive people of their due.

questions.text: What was the 'Day of the Shadow' punishment?
questions.answer: A cloud that provided shade from the heat but then rained down fire/punishment on the people of Madyan.

questions.text: Who brought down the Quran to the heart of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: The Trustworthy Spirit (Jibril/Gabriel).

questions.text: In what language was the Quran revealed to be clear to its people?
questions.answer: In a clear Arabic tongue.

questions.text: Was the Quran mentioned in the scriptures of former peoples?
questions.answer: Yes, 'And indeed, it is in the scriptures of former peoples.'

questions.text: What was a 'sign' for the disbelieving Quraysh that the Quran was true?
questions.answer: That the scholars of the Children of Israel recognized it.

questions.text: If the Quran had been revealed to a non-Arab in a foreign language, would the Quraysh have believed?
questions.answer: No, they still would not have believed in it.

questions.text: What is the Quranic response to the claim that Devils (Shayatin) brought down the Quran?
questions.answer: It is not suited for them, they are not able to do it, and they are removed from even hearing it.

questions.text: What instruction did Allah give the Prophet (SAW) regarding his relatives?
questions.answer: To warn his closest kindred.

questions.text: How should the Prophet treat the believers who follow him?
questions.answer: He should lower his wing (be kind and humble) to them.

questions.text: What should the Prophet say if his relatives disobey him?
questions.answer: 'Indeed, I am innocent of what you are doing.'

questions.text: Upon whom do the devils descend according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Upon every sinful liar.

questions.text: Why is the Surah named 'The Poets'?
questions.answer: Because it distinguishes the Prophet from poets, noting that poets are followed by the deviant and wander in every valley of speech.

questions.text: Which poets are exempted from the general criticism at the end?
questions.answer: Those who believe, do righteous deeds, remember Allah much, and defend themselves after being wronged.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the word 'Ash-Shu'ara'?
questions.answer: The Poets.

questions.text: What is the 'clear Book' mentioned in the opening?
questions.answer: The Quran.

questions.text: What is the 'noble pair' mentioned regarding plants?
questions.answer: That Allah grows on the earth every kind of noble pair of vegetation as a sign.

questions.text: What is the 'straitened breast' Musa feared?
questions.answer: A feeling of anxiety and inability to express himself clearly under pressure.

questions.text: What is the 'tongue' issue Musa asked Allah to help with?
questions.answer: He asked that his tongue be fluent so people could understand his message.

questions.text: What is the 'charge' Pharaoh held against Musa?
questions.answer: The accidental killing of an Egyptian man before he fled to Madyan.

questions.text: What is the 'favor' Pharaoh claimed to have done for Musa?
questions.answer: Raising him in his palace and providing for him.

questions.text: What was Musa's response to Pharaoh's 'favor' claim?
questions.answer: He said it was a favor only because Pharaoh had enslaved the Children of Israel.

questions.text: What did Pharaoh threaten to do to Musa if he took a god other than him?
questions.answer: He threatened to make him among the prisoners.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of the Festival' (Yawm al-Zinah)?
questions.answer: The day appointed for the public contest between Musa and the sorcerers.

questions.text: What is the 'laden ship' (Al-Fulk al-Mashhun)?
questions.answer: The Ark of Nuh which was full of people and animals.

questions.text: What is the 'reputation of honor' (Lisan Sidq)?
questions.answer: A legacy of being remembered truthfully and positively by future generations.

questions.text: What is the 'Sound Heart' (Qalbun Salim)?
questions.answer: A heart free from shirk, doubt, and spiritual diseases.

questions.text: What is the 'Shadow' (Zullah) in the context of the punishment of Madyan?
questions.answer: A cloud that appeared to provide relief but was actually the source of their destruction.

questions.text: What is the 'Trustworthy Spirit' (Ar-Ruh al-Amin)?
questions.answer: The Angel Jibril (Gabriel).

questions.text: What is the 'clear Arabic tongue' (Lisanin Arabiyyin Mubin)?
questions.answer: The language of the Quran which is easy to understand and eloquent.

questions.text: Who are the 'scholars of the Children of Israel' mentioned?
questions.answer: The sincere ones among them who knew the truth of the coming Prophet from their own scriptures.

questions.text: What is the 'incitement' of the devils?
questions.answer: The way they inspire falsehood and sin in the hearts of people.

questions.text: What is the 'Lowering of the wing'?
questions.answer: A metaphor for being humble, gentle, and compassionate toward others.

questions.text: What is the 'Exalted in Might' (Al-Aziz) attribute?
questions.answer: Allah's supreme power and authority.

questions.text: What is the 'Merciful' (Ar-Rahim) attribute?
questions.answer: Allah's deep compassion and kindness toward His creation.

questions.text: What is the 'Sign' (Ayah) mentioned after every story?
questions.answer: A lesson or proof of Allah's power and the truth of His messengers.

questions.text: Why were the 'strongholds' of 'Ad criticized?
questions.answer: Because they built them out of arrogance, thinking they would live forever.

questions.text: What is the 'Skill' of the people of Thamud?
questions.answer: Their architectural ability to carve intricate homes into solid rock.

questions.text: What was the 'designated day' for the She-Camel?
questions.answer: A day when she alone would drink from the well, and the people would drink on other days.

questions.text: Why did the people of Lut dislike 'purity'?
questions.answer: Because it exposed and condemned their own immoral lifestyles.

questions.text: What is the 'even balance' (Qistas al-Mustaqim)?
questions.answer: Fairness and justice in all business and social dealings.

questions.text: What is the 'Repentance' mentioned for the poets?
questions.answer: Turning back to Allah and using their talents for good after having done wrong.

questions.text: What is the 'Conclusion' (Munqalab) mentioned at the very end?
questions.answer: The ultimate destination and return of the wrongdoers to Allah's judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'Messenger of the Lord of the worlds' title?
questions.answer: The status Musa claimed when addressing Pharaoh.

questions.text: What is the 'Shining white hand' miracle?
questions.answer: Musa's hand becoming brilliantly white as a sign from Allah.

questions.text: What was the 'Magic' of the sorcerers?
questions.answer: A visual deception produced with ropes and staffs.

questions.text: What is the 'laden ship'?
questions.answer: The Ark which carried the seeds of a new humanity.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Day' (Yawm al-Azim) punishment?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment or the day of a specific nation's destruction.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' (Haqq) which Allah is?
questions.answer: The absolute and only true Deity.

questions.text: What is the 'Record' (Dhikr) of the ancients?
questions.answer: The history and scriptures of past civilizations.

questions.text: What is the 'Trustworthy' (Amin) quality of the messengers?
questions.answer: Their honesty and reliability in delivering Allah's message.

questions.text: What is the final warning to the wrongdoers?
questions.answer: That they will soon know to what kind of return they will return.
